title: Boat Rental in Varna Costs: A 2026 Guide to Prices and Options
html:
Boat rental in Varna on the Bulgarian Black Sea coast typically starts around 150 EUR for a half-day motorboat from the Sea Garden Marina, and you can find everything from budget shared tours to luxury crewed yachts through BoatHire24. The variety of vessels and the stunning bay make this a compelling destination for a day afloat.

Where to set off in Varna

Most rental boats depart from the Marina Primorski Park, the main harbour close to the city centre, or from the modern Varna Yacht Port near the Asparuhovo district. Further east, the Cape Galata area offers a scenic launch point with immediate access to open water. For those exploring the coast, the historic town of Balchik, 35 nautical miles north, and the resort of St. Constantine and Helena are popular stops.

You can also start from the smaller port at Kamchia, which is closer to the southern beaches, but the most reliable facilities are in the central marinas. The bay itself is sheltered, making it suitable for novice skippers, while the open sea beyond the cape tests more experienced crews.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests) Ideal for a couple of hours exploring the coast near St. Constantine. From 150 EUR for a half-day.
  2. RIB or speedboat (6-8 guests) Great for water sports and quick trips to the protected area of Yaylata. From 250 EUR per half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (6-10 guests) Perfect for a relaxing full-day cruise to Balchik Palace. From 500 EUR per day, skipper included.
  4. Catamaran (8-12 guests) Spacious for a group celebration, often anchored near the Golden Sands resort. From 900 EUR for a full day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (up to 12 guests) The Azimut 58, for example, offers comfort and speed; consider the Azimut 58 for hire if you want a premium experience. From 1,400 EUR for a full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Varna?

Prices vary by vessel type, season, and duration, but a typical half-day motorboat costs 150-300 EUR and a full-day crewed yacht starts around 800 EUR. The high season runs from June to September, when demand peaks and the local north-easterly wind, known as 'briesa', can make the sea choppier in the afternoon.

  • Shared boat tour (2-3 hours): 40-70 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 150-250 EUR.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, skipper included): 800-1,200 EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (up to 12 guests): from 1,200 EUR.

For a comprehensive listing of available vessels, you can rent a boat in Varna through the platform and compare verified options directly.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Varna?

For most bareboat charters of motorboats under 6m or low-powered craft, the operator provides a quick briefing and no formal licence is required for experienced adults. Larger yachts and sailing boats usually require an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent, though many operators include a skipper in the price. Always confirm with the owner before booking.

A local tip from Varna Yacht Port: book a morning trip to avoid the afternoon wind, and moor at Cape Galata for the clearest water near the city.

How booking works on BoatHire24

The process is straightforward: search for your dates, compare vessels, and book directly with the owner. Each listing includes photos, specs, and cancellation policy. For a step-by-step guide, see how boat rental works on the site.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150 EUR (half-day motorboat) to 1,400 EUR (full-day luxury yacht)
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Typical trip length: 4-8 hours or full day
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 12 people depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR (quoted by most local operators)
  • Licence: Usually not required for small boats; skipper available

Questions about boat rental in Varna

Can I rent a boat in Varna without prior experience?

Yes, many operators offer a skipper service or provide a short briefing for small motorboats, so complete beginners can enjoy the bay safely.

What is the best boat for a family trip in Varna?

A sailing yacht or a catamaran with a skipper is ideal, offering stability, shade, and enough space for children to move around comfortably.

Are there any hidden fees when renting a boat in Varna?

Most listings include fuel, mooring, and insurance, but you should always check the owner's terms for extras such as port fees or cleaning charges.

Can I take the boat across to Balchik for the day?

Yes, the 35-nautical-mile route takes about 1.5 hours by motorboat, and Balchik's marina welcomes day visitors; check with the owner for range and return time.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Varna or list your own. Whether you visit the city in 2026 for a short getaway or a longer exploration, the Black Sea coast offers reliable winds and sheltered anchorages. If you own a boat in Varna, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach a global audience; check availability on the homepage for details.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Varna and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
